understanding.goal_understanding: Get any pushable object (the selected piece or any pushable block) to reach or exceed the target_x coordinate.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions displace the selected piece by 3 units in the specified direction (MOVE_UP: y -= 3, MOVE_RIGHT: x += 3) without resistance. No pushing of blocks or wall collisions observed in these turns. Each action consumes 1 step from the budget. Scenario ended after 2 turns, likely due to reaching target_x with the selected piece.

orient_history: Sokoban-like grid puzzle with 3-unit (cell) cardinal moves. Snap to grid centers. Blocks/pushables movable if target clear; walls immovable. right_blocked indicates push impossible (pb can't move right into wt). Open up/down/left allow y-change to bypass y=0 obstacles. Win: any pushable (sel or pb) x>=6 regardless of y.

Grid-based world with 3-pixel cells (positions are left/top edges, moves shift by 3 pixels). Controllables (selected piece) and blocks are pushable; goal met when any's x >= 6. Walls block movement/pushing. Currently above the block/wall row (y=-3 vs y=0), open path rightward along y=-3 to bypass obstacles and reach x=6 directly with selected piece. No collisions on right moves from here.

2D pixel-grid world (3 pixels=1 cell/step). Controllables ('sel') move 3 units/cardinal dir to *_target if not blocked. Blocks/walls block movement unless pushed (adjacent + move into). Any pushable (sel/blocks) reaching x>=target_x wins. Efficient 3-unit cell moves; down blocked by 'pb', right/up/left open. Scenario 'transfer_wall_push' likely involves pushing block past wall, but direct sel right wins immediately.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_UP | Bypass pb/wt via y=-3: MOVE_UP to [0,-3] (open), then MOVE_RIGHT to [3,-3], MOVE_RIGHT to [6,-3] (sel.x=6 wins). 3 steps efficient.
history.observe: Initial state: sel at [0,0], pb at [3,0], wt at [6,0]. right_blocked=true due to adjacent_right pb; other directions unblocked. Target_x=6.
history.orient: Sokoban-like grid puzzle with 3-unit (cell) cardinal moves. Snap to grid centers. history.decide: Bypass pb/wt via y=-3: MOVE_UP to [0,-3] (open), then MOVE_RIGHT to [3,-3], MOVE_RIGHT to [6,-3] (sel.x=6 wins). 3 steps efficient.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | MOVE_RIGHT to [3, -3]; positions selected piece at x=3 (still <6). Next turn MOVE_RIGHT to [6, -3] for x=6 win. Efficient (2 more steps, total 3/10); avoids potential block/wall push issues.
history.observe: Selected piece moved up from [0, 0] to [0, -3] as a result of previous MOVE_UP. No other changes; block still at [3, 0], wall at [6, 0]. No adjacencies detected. Steps remaining: 9.
history.orient: Grid-based world with 3-pixel cells (positions are left/top edges, moves shift by 3 pixels). Controllables (selected piece) and blocks are pushable; goal met when any's x >= 6. Walls block movement/pushing. Currently above the block/wall row (y=-3 vs y=0), open path rightward along y=-3 to bypass obstacles and reach x=6 directly with selected piece. No collisions on right moves from here.
history.decide: MOVE_RIGHT to [3, -3]; positions selected piece at x=3 (still <6). Next turn MOVE_RIGHT to [6, -3] for x=6 win. Efficient (2 more steps, total 3/10); avoids potential block/wall push issues.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | MOVE_RIGHT: advances sel to [6, -3] (x=6>=target_x), wins instantly. Right clear, no blocks/walls in path. Ignores pb for now as direct win cheaper than pushing.
history.observe: Selected piece moved right from [0, -3] to [3, -3]. Now adjacent down to block 'pb' at [3, 0], down_blocked=true. Right_target=[6, -3] clear (right_blocked=false, no adjacent right). Wall 'wt' at [6, 0].
